novel_features.name: Token-lean doc projection
novel_features.description: Project a 50KB+ DocC JSON page down to just the fields an agent actually needs — abstract, signature, platforms, or all three — saving context tokens on every lookup.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local parsed cache and shape-aware renderer; competitors return full payloads. Only possible with our offline SQLite store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is grounding code generation against an Apple symbol and only needs the abstract + signature + platform floor, not the full discussion + see-also tree.

novel_features.name: Cross-platform replacement finder
novel_features.description: For a symbol unavailable on a target platform, walk the See-Also / Replacement-Of graph until landing on an alternative that IS available there and is not itself deprecated.
novel_features.rationale: Walks the local reference graph; no Apple endpoint answers 'what replaces this symbol on visionOS' directly.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when porting code between Apple platforms (iPad → visionOS, AppKit → SwiftUI, deprecated → current) and you need the migration target, not just a similar-named API.

novel_features.name: Framework snapshot diff
novel_features.description: Diff two stored framework index snapshots and classify each delta as added, removed, deprecated, or likely-renamed (path-stem similarity).
novel_features.rationale: Requires two local snapshots; no Apple endpoint returns a structured diff.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use after every WWDC or dot-release to surface added/removed/deprecated symbols at the framework level.

novel_features.name: Deprecation cliff report
novel_features.description: List every Apple API deprecated in a given platform version, grouped by framework and symbol kind, with the replacement-hint column joined from references.
novel_features.rationale: SQL aggregation over Apple's uniquely structured `metadata.platforms[].deprecatedAt` field; not possible from any single Apple endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when planning a migration sprint or writing a 'what's deprecated this year' guide; the only way to get the full list in one shot.

novel_features.name: Conformance graph
novel_features.description: Walk a framework's `relationshipsSections` to enumerate concrete conformers of a protocol and the protocol's ancestors.
novel_features.rationale: Exploits DocC's structured `relationshipsSections` rows; competitors return untyped See-Also lists and miss conformance edges entirely.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when writing protocol-driven code (custom View, ObservableObject, Layout) and you need every concrete type that conforms.

novel_features.name: Cross-framework symbol grep
novel_features.description: Regex over every synced framework's symbols with filters on kind, target platform, and deprecation status.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-framework regex requires unified local FTS; Dash/Zeal only grep within one docset and online competitors offer keyword-only search.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when you remember the shape of a symbol name but not where it lives, or when auditing API patterns across the whole Apple SDK.

novel_features.name: WWDC reverse index
novel_features.description: For a WWDC session ID, list every symbol whose doc page cites that session.
novel_features.rationale: Joins `references` rows where `kind=video` back to symbol pages; competitors index videos but not the reverse video → symbols edge.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use after watching a WWDC session to enumerate every API it touched, or to find which session officially introduced an API you're working with.

novel_features.name: Agent-shape doc bundle
novel_features.description: Bundle a symbol's Markdown plus its depth-1 See-Also pages into a single token-budgeted blob, ready to paste into an agent prompt.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the symbol with its reference graph and applies a token budget; sosumi.ai proves Markdown demand but ships single-page renders only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs a self-contained context blob about a symbol plus its closest relatives, without doing N round-trips and N JSON parses.

narrative.headline: Every Apple framework, indexed locally, with deprecation analysis and an MCP server no other Apple-docs tool has.
narrative.value_prop: apple-docs-pp-cli mirrors developer.apple.com's DocC JSON into a local SQLite store you can grep across every framework, diff between releases, and project down to just the fields an agent needs. Ships with offline FTS, a cross-platform 'port-to' walker, a deprecation-cliff report, and an MCP server you can plug into Claude Desktop.
narrative.auth_narrative: No auth required. The DocC JSON endpoints under https://developer.apple.com/tutorials/data/ are public and served by Apple's CDN.

quickstart.comment: Health check — verify network reachability without writing local state.
Cache the master list of every Apple framework locally.
Regex over a framework's index — finds every symbol whose title or path matches.
Token-lean projection: just the declaration fragments, not the full 50KB page.
Every SwiftUI API Apple deprecated in iOS 18.

troubleshoots.symptom: 404 from developer.apple.com for a framework slug
troubleshoots.fix: Slugs are case-sensitive lowercase with no separators. 'photokit' works; 'photoskit' returns 404. Try 'apple-docs-pp-cli technologies list' to list valid slugs.
Pass --framework <slug> to focus the scan, or raise --max-scan-frameworks; grep fetches one index per framework.

troubleshoots.symptom: Index sync is slow for SwiftUI/Foundation
troubleshoots.fix: Framework indices are 500KB–2MB; pass '--resources index --max-pages 1' on first sync, then add frameworks one at a time.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when you need to read Apple's docs from a terminal, when an agent is grounding Swift code generation against current Apple APIs, when planning a platform migration (iPad → visionOS, AppKit → SwiftUI), or when authoring a 'what's deprecated in iOS N' guide. The local SQLite store unlocks queries — cross-framework grep, snapshot diff, deprecation cliff — that no online docs viewer offers.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to author Apple ID or App Store Connect API calls — use a separate App Store Connect CLI for those.
Do not use this CLI as a Swift package manager — it reads docs, not source.
Do not use this CLI to fetch full WWDC video transcripts — it indexes WWDC references from doc pages but does not host video content.

recipes.title: Find a symbol's iOS introduction version
recipes.explanation: Returns just the platform-availability map — under 500 bytes vs 9KB for the full page.

recipes.title: List every concrete SwiftUI type that conforms to View
recipes.explanation: Walks the local relationshipsSections graph; impossible from any single Apple endpoint.

recipes.title: What changed in SwiftUI at this year's WWDC?
recipes.explanation: Classifies every delta as added / removed / deprecated / likely-renamed in one call.

recipes.title: Find the visionOS replacement for a UIKit symbol
recipes.explanation: Walks See-Also and Replacement-Of edges until landing on a symbol available on visionOS.

recipes.title: Bundle a symbol's docs as agent context
recipes.explanation: Markdown render of the symbol + every depth-1 See-Also page, truncated to a token budget.

trigger_phrases: port this from UIKit to SwiftUI
